embeddings.content:
Mixed Format Engineering Notes

Summary
This file exists as a noisy-but-readable fixture for document search. It should be
different from the cleaner markdown guides, but it should still produce English snippets
that are easy to inspect during manual testing.

Experience Snapshot
- Built a RAG assistant for internal engineering documentation.
- Added retrieval, reranking, and prompt grounding to improve answer quality.
- Exposed search diagnostics so engineers could inspect top matches and latency.

Project Highlights
1. Search quality matters more than raw recall when the system will summarize results.
2. RAG pipelines benefit from clean snippets that preserve sentence boundaries.
3. Mixed-format source files should still produce readable tool outputs.

Implementation Notes
The retrieval layer used embeddings for candidate generation and a heuristic reranker for
better ordering. The agent workflow then decided whether it should answer directly, call a
tool, or ask the user for clarification before continuing.

Observability Checklist
- Log the incoming query.
- Log the returned document set.
- Log the top match reason.
- Log latency for search, planning, and execution.

Resume Fragment
Designed and implemented a document search workflow that supports top-N result limits,
filename filters, and short supporting summaries for downstream ticket creation.

Mixed Formatting Block
Section: Search Summary
Key takeaway -> RAG combines retrieval with generation to improve factual grounding.
Note: Reranking can improve result quality when several documents mention the same topic.
